name: cable standing cross-over high reverse fly
instructions: Attach a D-handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height.
Stand in the middle of the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, palms facing forward.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise.
Engage your shoulder muscles and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles towards the front of your body.
Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Attach a D-handle to each side of a cable machine at shoulder height. Stand in the middle of the cable machine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the handles with an overhand grip and extend your arms out to the sides, palms facing forward. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and maintain a straight back throughout the exercise. Engage your shoulder muscles and squeeze your shoulder blades together as you pull the handles towards the front of your body. Pause for a moment at the peak of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
